title: Roots

cover.alt: Book cover in shades of brown and white, reading 'ROOTS / The Saga of an American Family / ALEX HALEY

overview: Alex Haley's 'saga of an American family' spans multiple generations, from Africa, to slavery in the American South, to the 1970s.  The Pulitzer-prize winning novel was adapted into a popular TV miniseries, and inspired many Americans to trace their own family roots.

incidents.description: The Knox County school district {ln:https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/may/15/tennessee-book-ban-alex-haley-roots|removed this book from library shelves} under state law. A spokesperson for the district {ln:https://www.rawstory.com/tennessee-bans-roots/|cited a single passage in the novel's 84th chapter}, in which a 16-year old is raped by her enslaver. District officials said that met Tennessee's legal criteria for 'sadomasochistic abuse' that required the book's removal from all district libraries. 'Broader themes or historical significance of a work as a whole is not a consideration under the law', the official stated. But the law's disregard for such significance makes it easy for anyone objecting to books depicting the unvarnished reality of slavery, in which rape was common, to target them for removal.
